ocr.text: In Q1 2024, Munich Re's distribution channel mix revealed significant performance metrics across various categories. The total
premiums collected were CHF115M, with claims paid amounting to CHF43M. Direct sales generated CHF45M in premiums
and CHF20M in claims paid, accounting for 40% of total premiums.

PremiumsCollected  ClaimsPaid
DirectSales  CHF45M  CHF20M
Brokerage  CHF30M  CHF10M  OnlineSales
CHF25M  CHF5M  Partnerships  CHF15M  CHF8M  Total
CHF115M  CHF43M  CHF30M  CHF20M  CHF12M  CHF10M
DirectSales  40%  Brokerage  25%  OnlineSales  15%
Partnerships  10%  Others  10%  PremiumGrowth  8%
ClaimsRatio  37%  DirectSales  45%  Brokerage  15%
OnlineSales  20%  Partnerships  10%  Others  5%
AveragePremium  CHF1,000  CustomerGrowth  4,500  TotalPolicies  25,000
MarketShare  12%  RetentionRate  90%  NetPromoterScore  45
The brokerage channel contributed CHF30M in premiums with CHF10M in claims paid, reflecting 25% of the total premiums.
Online sales accounted for CHF25M in premiums and CHF5M in claims paid, representing 15% of the total premiums.
Partnerships generated CHF15M in premiums with CHF8M in claims paid, making up 10% of the total. Others accounted for
CHF12M in premiums and CHF10M in claims paid, also 10% of total premiums. The overall premium growth was 8%, and the
claims ratio stood at 37%. The claims ratio for direct sales was 45%, while brokerage recorded a 15% ratio. Online sales had a
claims ratio of 20%, and partnerships maintained a claims ratio of 10%, with others at 5%. The average premium was
CHF1,000, and customer growth reached 4,500, leading to a total of 25,000 policies. Market share was recorded at 12%, with a
retention rate of 90% and a net promoter score of 45. This comprehensive evaluation of the distribution channels underscores
the robust performance of Munich Re in a competitive market landscape.
